data.description: Zodiac system based on the equinoxes and seasons, commonly used in modern Western astrology.
data.usage_note: Default reference system for most Western natal, daily and interpretive calculations.

data.description: Zodiac system based on the fixed-star background, commonly used in Vedic and some traditional astrological systems.
data.usage_note: Requires an ayanamsha to define the offset from the tropical zodiac.

data.description: Zodiac system calculated by placing the lunar North Node at 0° Aries.
data.usage_note: Used mainly for karmic, nodal or soul-oriented interpretive layers.

data.description: Reference frame calculated from the perspective of Earth.
data.usage_note: Standard frame for most astrology chart calculations.

data.description: Reference frame calculated from the perspective of the Sun.
data.usage_note: Used for specialized astrological or astronomical analysis, not for standard natal interpretation.

data.description: Reference frame calculated from a specific location on Earth.
data.usage_note: Useful when observer location materially affects positions, especially for parallax-sensitive bodies such as the Moon.

data.description: Astronomical coordinate system based on right ascension and declination.
data.usage_note: Useful for storing raw astronomical star data.

data.description: Coordinate system based on the plane of the ecliptic, using ecliptic longitude and latitude.
data.usage_note: Useful for astrological longitude calculations and fixed-star projections.
